this_week_focus: Michael: convene with Youdi by July 20 to draft answers for Lorcan Clarke's second DD round (due July 31) on cost-effectiveness edge cases and Mtwara RCT power—USD 5M Founders Pledge decision hinges on these technical responses.

top_3_actions.action: Michael + Youdi: 90-min working session by July 20 to anticipate and draft responses to likely second-round DD questions: (1) cost-effectiveness sensitivity analysis for SOMA USD 3.12/student/year model under government subsidy scenarios; (2) RCT statistical power justification for Mtwara n=70 sample; (3) government sustainability pathway post-pilot phase-out. Prepare brief proactive email to Lorcan by July 28 flagging any gaps you identify in June submission.

Registration deadline Sept 30 is hard stop—11.5 weeks away. Missing this window delays Timamu by 12+ months and forces reliance on slower rolling grants (Microsoft, Adobe). Act immediately or lose strategic opportunity.

pipeline_health.soma: Founders Pledge USD 5M in active second DD phase (decision Q1-Q2 2027); Dovetail Track 2 (USD 150K, Ali Ellegard) and VTF SOMA conversation (USD 150K, technology mandate fit) still dormant—start Ali by Sept 1, wait for VTF contract signature (~July 30) before SOMA pitch. Microsoft AI for Accessibility (USD 150K, rolling Oct 1) and Adobe Foundation (USD 50K, rolling Sept 1) are backup anchors. Legal registration status must be confirmed TODAY.
pipeline_health.concentration_risk: Founders Pledge USD 5M represents 58% of SOMA gap (USD 637K total gap / USD 3.12 per student locked cost). If FP decision slips to H2 2027 or declines, SOMA faces critical shortfall. Dovetail + VTF + GPE conversations must accelerate in parallel to diversify SOMA funding profile.
pipeline_health.gap_progress: 2027 SOMA gap remains USD 637K (confirmed USD 229K / needed USD 866K). Founders Pledge second DD round will determine realistic 2027 acquisition probability. If FP progresses to final round (likely), gap reduces to ~USD 137K—achievable through Dovetail (USD 150K) or VTF (USD 150K). If FP declines, gap climbs to USD 787K and requires GPE + government co-funding urgently. No material gap movement until FP second round closes (late July) or progresses (Aug+).

positioning_move: Michael: Publish 1-minute LinkedIn post this week highlighting LearnImpact's 10+ year evidence legacy + May 2026 independence from Twaweza. Frame as: 'After a decade of partnership with Twaweza, LearnImpact is now an independent NGO (Reg. 00NGO/R/8931) driving AI-enabled foundational learning and creator economy inclusion in East Africa. Our SOMA programme has locked cost-effectiveness of USD 3.12/student/year. Excited to announce new partnerships ahead.' This signals maturity, independence credibility, and cost discipline to Founders Pledge, Hempel, and Tanzania government audiences simultaneously. Time publication for July 21 (after Youdi session, before Lorcan's second questions).

flags.flag: Founders Pledge USD 5M second DD questions (expected July 31) could surface cost-effectiveness challenges around Mtwara RCT n=70 statistical power or government sustainability post-pilot. If FP interprets RCT as underpowered, deal probability drops significantly.
flags.response: Michael + Youdi session by July 20. Prepare pre-emptive brief to Lorcan by July 28 addressing statistical power justification and post-pilot sustainability modelling. If power is genuinely weak, consider alternative framing (pilot learning vs. validation RCT) or government co-investment pathway.
